digest.component_or_area: completion-verifier close gate / verdict sidecar persistence
propositions: Completion-verifier FAIL verdicts are not persisted durably, so only PASS attestations survive and FAILs must be reconstructed from voluntary session-log narration.
FAIL verdicts should be written as a reducer-ignored sidecar event in the ticket store (mirroring the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT pattern) so they are queryable offline.

The persisted record must carry schema="completion_verifier_fail_v1", the failing criteria, per-finding remediation text, verdict, findings, certifiable, model/runner, timestamp, and material fingerprint.
The persistence must be best-effort inside a try/except before the FAIL is raised, so a write failure does not mask or alter the fail-closed blocking behavior.
Tests drive a mocked FAIL through the close path and assert the record exists with expected schema, criteria, and remediation fields.
A persistence-failure branch test mocks the sidecar write to raise and asserts the close path still blocks with the FAIL verdict unchanged.
